text: https://openspec.dev/ add one of these for the project

discussion: https://github.com/Mic92/dotfiles/pull/4786

Migrate from flake-parts to adios-flake by Mic92 · Pull Request #4786 · Mic92/dotfiles - GitHub
Replace flake-parts with adios-flake, a lighter-weight alternative that uses direct function dispatch instead of the NixOS module system for per-system evaluation. What changed flake.nix: replaced...
https://github.com/Mic92/adios-flakE

Fixed. The mock netcfg-setup package was missing pname, so parseDrvName "mock-netcfg-setup" returned "mock-netcfg-setup"
 instead of "netcfg-setup", which didn't match the bootEssentialNames list. This caused it to be placed in the managed profile
 (/nix/system/profile/bin/) instead of flat-copied to usr/bin/, failing the artifact test.

 The cargo test failure (cannot find -lc) is the documented pre-existing cross-compilation limitation — not caused by any of
 these changes.

 Error: ❌ Auto-fix exhausted (3 attempts): cargo test, nix flake check

C Library Foundation — Built ✅

 ┌─────────┬──────────────┬───────┬────────────────────────┐
 │ Library │ Version      │ Size  │ What it unlocks        │
 ├─────────┼──────────────┼───────┼────────────────────────┤
 │ zlib    │ 1.3.1        │ 167KB │ libpng, curl, git,     │
 │         │              │       │ python, everything     │
 ├─────────┼──────────────┼───────┼────────────────────────┤
 │ zstd    │ 1.5.7        │ 2.5MB │ LLVM, build bridge NAR │
 │         │              │       │ compression            │
 ├─────────┼──────────────┼───────┼────────────────────────┤
 │ expat   │ 2.5.0        │ 353KB │ dbus, fontconfig, git, │
 │         │              │       │ python                 │
 ├─────────┼──────────────┼───────┼────────────────────────┤
 │ OpenSSL │ 1.1.1 (Redox │ 5.2MB │ curl, git, openssh,    │
 │         │ fork)        │       │ python, TLS            │
 └─────────┴──────────────┴───────┴────────────────────────┘

 ### Infrastructure created

 - mk-c-library.nix — Cross-compilation builder with:
     - mkLibrary — base builder with full cross-env setup
     - mkAutotools — ./configure && make && make install
     - mkCmake — CMake with cross-compilation toolchain
     - Uses clang + relibc sysroot, produces static .a +
 headers

 ### What this unlocks next

 With zlib + openssl + expat available as buildInputs, we
 can now cross-compile:
 - curl (needs zlib + openssl) → HTTP client
 - git (needs zlib + openssl + expat) → version control
 - libpng (needs zlib) → image support → freetype2 →
 fontconfig → graphics stack
 - ncurses + readline → bash, vim, htop, nano
 - Any Rust crate with -sys dependencies on these (e.g.,
 openssl-sys, zlib-sys)
